site.name: PurrNet Docs
site.tagline: Interactive documentation for PurrNet — Unity's purrfect multiplayer networking library.

home.heroDesc: Interactive animations for every command. Understand how multiplayer networking works — not just the API, but the why and when.

zeroBoilerplate.label: Zero Boilerplate
zeroBoilerplate.desc: Instantiate() and Destroy() just work. No special spawn calls.

flexibleAuthority.label: Flexible Authority
flexibleAuthority.desc: Switch between client auth and server auth via Network Rules — no code changes.

modularDesign.label: Modular Design
modularDesign.desc: NetworkModules enable reusable network logic independent of MonoBehaviours.

perComponentOwnership.label: Per-Component Ownership
perComponentOwnership.desc: A single GameObject can have different owners per NetworkIdentity component.

richSyncTypes.label: Rich Sync Types
richSyncTypes.desc: SyncVar, SyncList, SyncDictionary, SyncEvent, SyncTimer, SyncInput.

rpcSuperset.label: RPC Superset
rpcSuperset.desc: ServerRpc, ObserversRpc, TargetRpc — plus awaitable, generic, and static variants.

comingSoon.title: Full content coming soon
comingSoon.body: Detailed interactive documentation for this topic is being written. For now, refer to the official PurrNet GitBook.

gitRequired.title: Git required
gitRequired.body: Make sure Git is installed on your system before using the Package Manager URL method. After installing Git, restart Unity and Unity Hub.

whoCanSpawn.title: Who can spawn?
whoCanSpawn.body: By default (Server Auth rules), only the server can spawn networked objects. With Client Auth rules, clients can also call Instantiate() for networked prefabs. Control this via Network Rules.

runLocallyDivergence.title: RunLocally divergence
runLocallyDivergence.body: When using runLocally: true, the client may briefly show a state that the server later corrects. Always reconcile server authoritative state on the client to prevent visual glitches.

neverTrustClientInput.title: Never trust client input
neverTrustClientInput.body: Always validate on the server. A client can send any value — always check bounds, cooldowns, and game state before applying the result.

bufferLastVsSyncVar.title: bufferLast vs SyncVar
bufferLastVsSyncVar.body: For a value that changes continuously (health, score, position), prefer a SyncVar — it handles buffering automatically. Use bufferLast on ObserversRpc for one-time events that must be replayed (game start, round end).

requireServer.title: requireServer
requireServer.body: If requireServer is false (default), clients can directly call ObserversRpc themselves if Network Rules allow it. In server-authoritative games, always set requireServer: true.

declareAsField.title: Declare in field, not in methods
declareAsField.body: SyncVar must be declared as a class field (or property) on a NetworkBehaviour. Creating one inside a method or adding it to a list will break synchronisation.

avoidSyncEveryFrame.title: Avoid syncing every frame
avoidSyncEveryFrame.body: Don't set .value in Update() without a change check. Always check if the value actually changed before assigning.

ownerAuthAndServer.title: ownerAuth and server writes
ownerAuthAndServer.body: When ownerAuth: true, only the owner can write from a client. The server can always write regardless.

setDirtyForStructs.title: SetDirty for mutable structs
setDirtyForStructs.body: If T is a struct and you mutate a field of an element in place, the SyncList cannot detect the change automatically. Call SetDirty(index) after the mutation to force a sync.

isControllerShortcut.title: IsController shortcut
isControllerShortcut.body: Use IsController instead of isOwner || isServer — it returns true if you are the owner, or if you are the server and the object has no owner.

giveOwnershipServerSide.title: GiveOwnership is server-side
giveOwnershipServerSide.body: Call GiveOwnership from server code only unless your Network Rules explicitly allow client-initiated ownership transfer. Unrestricted ownership transfer is a common cheat vector.

unsafeInProduction.title: Never use Unsafe rules in production
unsafeInProduction.body: The Unsafe preset gives all clients full authority. It is meant for rapid prototyping only. Ship with ServerOwner or ServerStrict.

pooledObjectReference.title: Reference<T> for pooled objects
pooledObjectReference.body: Serialized references to components on pooled objects can break after pool recycling. Use PurrNet's Reference<T> wrapper instead.

noOverrideOnRemote.title: Don't override transform directly on remote clients
noOverrideOnRemote.body: Setting transform.position directly on a non-authority client will be overwritten by the next NetworkTransform sync. Only modify position on the authority.

bandwidthOptimization.title: Bandwidth optimization
bandwidthOptimization.body: Disable sync axes you don't need. Use tolerance values to suppress micro-updates when the object is stationary.

triggerReliableChannel.title: Triggers need Reliable channel
triggerReliableChannel.body: Animator triggers are one-shot events. If the packet drops, the trigger is lost. Always use Channel.Reliable for trigger parameters.

kinematicBodies.title: Kinematic Rigidbodies
kinematicBodies.body: NetworkRigidbody is designed for dynamic Rigidbodies. For kinematic bodies, use NetworkTransform instead.

modulesNotInLists.title: Do not store modules in collections
modulesNotInLists.body: Network modules must be declared as fields or properties on a NetworkIdentity. Storing them in arrays, lists, or dictionaries will break network registration.

predictionComplexity.title: Prediction adds significant complexity
predictionComplexity.body: Client-side prediction requires deterministic physics and careful state management. Start with NetworkTransform and only add prediction when you have a specific responsiveness requirement.

neverStorePlainPasswords.title: Never store plain-text passwords
neverStorePlainPasswords.body: Always hash credentials before sending over the network. PurrNet encrypts transport data but you are responsible for credential security.

iPackedAutoVsManual.title: IPackedAuto vs IPacked
iPackedAutoVsManual.body: Use IPackedAuto for simple data structs. Use IPacked for fine-grained control, delta compression, or types with variable-length fields.

purrTransportFree.title: Purr Transport is free for development
purrTransportFree.body: The built-in Purr relay transport requires no setup and is free for testing. Switch to UDP or Steam for production.

introduction.title: Introduction to PurrNet
introduction.description: PurrNet is a free, MIT-licensed Unity networking library designed to feel native — no baking, no magic spawning APIs, no paywalls.

installation.title: Installation
installation.description: Three ways to add PurrNet to your Unity project.

minimalSetup.title: Minimal Setup
minimalSetup.description: Get a networked scene running in under 5 minutes.

networkIdentity.title: NetworkIdentity
networkIdentity.description: NetworkIdentity is the core component that registers a GameObject with the PurrNet network. Every networked object must have at least one.

networkBehaviour.title: NetworkBehaviour
networkBehaviour.description: NetworkBehaviour is the base class for all multiplayer scripts. It extends NetworkIdentity with RPC support, SyncVars, and lifecycle hooks.

ownership.title: Ownership
ownership.description: Ownership determines which client has authority to drive a networked object. PurrNet supports per-component ownership.

spawning.title: Spawning & Despawning
spawning.description: PurrNet intercepts Unity's Instantiate() and Destroy() calls — there are no special network spawn methods to learn.

networkRules.title: Network Rules
networkRules.description: Network Rules is a ScriptableObject that defines the authority model for your game — who can spawn, despawn, call RPCs, and write SyncVars.

serverRpc.title: ServerRpc
serverRpc.description: ServerRpc executes a method on the server when called from a client. It is the primary way for clients to send authoritative commands.

observersRpc.title: ObserversRpc
observersRpc.description: ObserversRpc broadcasts a method call from the server to all clients observing the object.

targetRpc.title: TargetRpc
targetRpc.description: TargetRpc sends a method call from the server to exactly one specific client, identified by their PlayerID.

awaitableRpc.title: Awaitable RPC
awaitableRpc.description: Awaitable RPCs let you await a return value from a remote method call, similar to async/await in regular C#.

staticRpc.title: Static & Generic RPC
staticRpc.description: PurrNet supports RPCs on static methods and generic type parameters for maximum flexibility.

syncVar.title: SyncVar
syncVar.description: SyncVar automatically synchronises a typed variable across all connected clients whenever its value changes.

syncList.title: SyncList
syncList.description: SyncList is a network-aware list that automatically synchronises additions, removals, insertions, and clears across all clients.

syncDictionary.title: SyncDictionary
syncDictionary.description: SyncDictionary is a network-aware key-value store that synchronises all mutations across all clients.

syncEvent.title: SyncEvent
syncEvent.description: SyncEvent is a networked event that fires across all clients when invoked on the authority — no manual RPC needed.

syncTimer.title: SyncTimer
syncTimer.description: SyncTimer is a synchronized countdown timer that stays in lockstep across all clients automatically.

syncInput.title: SyncInput
syncInput.description: SyncInput synchronises raw player input from the owning client to the server, enabling server-side physics processing.

networkTransform.title: Network Transform
networkTransform.description: NetworkTransform automatically synchronises position, rotation, scale, and parent across all clients.

networkAnimator.title: Network Animator
networkAnimator.description: NetworkAnimator synchronises Animator parameters and state transitions across all clients.

networkRigidbody.title: Network Rigidbody
networkRigidbody.description: NetworkRigidbody synchronises physics state (velocity, angular velocity) across the network so remote Rigidbodies behave physically.

broadcasts.title: Broadcasts
broadcasts.description: Broadcasts send arbitrary data packets at the connection level — not tied to any NetworkIdentity.

networkModules.title: Network Modules
networkModules.description: NetworkModule lets you create reusable, network-aware components that are not MonoBehaviours.

playerIdentity.title: PlayerIdentity
playerIdentity.description: PlayerIdentity is a typed registry that makes it easy to find, iterate, and call methods on all player objects.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: PurrNet authentication validates clients with custom payloads before allowing them to participate in the game.

bitpacker.title: BitPacker Serialization
bitpacker.description: BitPacker is PurrNet's efficient binary serializer for custom network types, minimising bandwidth.

transports.title: Transports
transports.description: PurrNet supports multiple transport layers: UDP, WebSocket, Steam, EOS, and more.

clientSidePrediction.title: Client-Side Prediction
clientSidePrediction.description: PurrDiction provides a full client-side prediction and server reconciliation framework built on top of PurrNet.

sections.whenToUse: When to use — and when not to
